element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns to set power, angle, and spin against single stationary enemy (Kabu)
Multiple enemies positioned progressively; player learns trajectory planning
Acquired by hitting specific enemies; enable shortcuts and obstacle destruction
Spark ability breaks certain barriers; UFO ability allows free movement over hazards
Final enemy transforms into star-goal; adds timing and sequencing pressure
Quality medals (gold/silver/bronze) based on performance; unlock alternate course versions
Players compete to change star ownership; Copy abilities used for direct combat

typical_relief_triggers: Successfully hitting target enemy and collecting Copy ability
Landing Kirby in goal hole, completing hole
Collecting medal and advancing to next course
typical_tension_triggers: Multiple enemies scattered across isometric space requiring precision
Ricochet shots through tight passages or around obstacles
Final enemy transformation into goal; must navigate new target
Multiplayer opponent stealing stars with Copy ability
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating per-course; early courses establish mechanics with forgiving layouts, mid-courses add spatial complexity and enemy density, late courses demand high precision and ability sequencing. Multiplayer introduces competitive rhythm with turn-taking and stealing mechanics.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Copy ability placement allows multiple solutions; courses designed with shortcuts accessible only via specific power-ups, encouraging replays and skill expression

difficulty_curve.early_game: Course 1 introduces core mechanics (aiming, power, spin, enemy collision) with straightforward enemy layouts and generous stroke counts. Player learns to acquire Copy abilities safely.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Courses 2-5 increase enemy density, introduce complex isometric spatial arrangements, and require ability sequencing for optimal play. Ricochet shots and obstacle destruction become necessary.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Courses 6-8 demand high precision, multiple ability combinations per hole, and tight stroke limits. Spatial complexity peaks; player must predict multi-bounce trajectories and enemy movements.
notable_spikes: Transition from Course 1 to Course 2: enemy placement density increases significantly
Courses 6-8: stroke limits tighten while course complexity peaks
Multiplayer mode: competitive stealing mechanics and turn-based pacing create different tension than single-player

notable_level_patterns.description: Each enemy hit grants a Copy ability and counts as progress toward goal activation. Players sequence enemy collisions to collect necessary abilities before final hole transformation.
Courses leverage isometric perspective to create multi-bounce trajectories where angle and spin precision determine success. Obstacles and enemy placement create natural 'lanes' for skilled play.
Copy abilities (especially UFO) unlock direct paths to goal, bypassing standard enemy sequences. Courses designed with both ability-dependent and ability-free solutions.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All eight courses contain ability-dependent shortcuts

notable_level_patterns.description: Unique multiplayer courses feature symmetric or mirrored layouts allowing both players equal strategic options. Star theft mechanics create push-pull rhythm of score control.
